user_query:
I want a tiny, local release pipeline I can run on my laptop in Kango Haraza to bundle and share simple text announcements with neighbors. Please create a bash script at scripts/release.sh that does the following and then run it so the outputs are produced:

1) One-time tool download: Download the official Linux x86_64 static binary of yq, version v4.44.3, from the "mikefarah/yq" GitHub Releases (identify the asset by repository, release tag v4.44.3, and Linux amd64 artifact name; if it comes as an archive, extract the binary). Save the executable as tools/yq and ensure it is runnable. Do not use any system-installed yq; use the downloaded one. Record the exact output of tools/yq --version in the report and fail if it does not include v4.44.3.

2) Version bump: Read the exact version string from input/version.txt (trim whitespace) and update the version field in input/app.yaml using tools/yq, in place.

3) Package: Read input/release_rules.json to determine which files to include/exclude. Create a zip file under dist/ named according to artifact_name_template (substitute {version} with the version from input/version.txt). The zip must contain:
   - The updated app.yaml placed at the root of the archive as app.yaml (not under the input/ folder).
   - All files matched by include_globs minus exclude_globs, but with the leading "input/" path segment stripped in the archive (e.g., input/content/announcements_public.txt becomes content/announcements_public.txt inside the zip).

4) Checksums and report: Compute the SHA-256 of the zip and write it to dist/checksums.txt as a single line in the form: artifact=<zip_name> sha256=<hex>. Also create build/report.json with exactly these fields:
   - yq_version: string (from tools/yq --version)
   - app_version: string (the version now in input/app.yaml after update)
   - artifact_path: string (e.g., dist/haraza-announcements-v0.3.0.zip)
   - artifact_size_bytes: integer
   - included_files: array of strings listing paths as they appear inside the zip, sorted ascending
   - excluded_files: array of strings listing the input/content/* files that were excluded by the rules, sorted ascending
   - zip_sha256: string (hex digest)

5) Constraints:
   - Do not hardcode a direct URL in the repo; locate the yq v4.44.3 Linux amd64 asset from its official Releases by the attributes above and download it at runtime.
   - The script must run in a basic Linux shell without Docker. You may call tools like Python or POSIX utilities if available.

Inputs are in the input/ directory already. Outputs must be created at:
   - dist/<artifact zip from template>
   - dist/checksums.txt
   - build/report.json

After running scripts/release.sh, verify that:
   - input/app.yaml shows the updated version matching input/version.txt
   - The dist zip exists and contains app.yaml at the root and only the allowed content files
   - build/report.json fields are consistent with the produced artifact and include the yq version All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
